Hello there and welcome. I want to also highlight in a little bit more detail why selling product, especially upon the initial or second visit, is so important from the liquidation of ad spend standpoint. Also, just to further invest your customers. And you need to break the fear of like, well, I feel like I'm selling them too much. It doesn't work that way. And you're going to limit yourself because ultimately, when someone signs up for something new is the moment that they're most excited to get invested in that thing. And so we owe it to them to treat them like we would treat ourselves, which most people when they start a new program, they go and get all their groceries, they go get all their supplements, all their products to go with it so that they can start and feel excited about it. So we need to capitalize on that so that we can provide more value and then ultimately make our business more profitable so that we can again solidify ourselves and solidify our employees and solidify all the things that we are doing this for. Okay. So this is just a quick run through that I just went over is that if for some reason you have some living beliefs, so let's say you're gym number one, and you haven't even done hybrid. All right. So you're just doing normal challenges, 20% margin, you're making $100 net per sale. So it's $20 per lead break even point. All right. There it is straightforward. That's it. Okay. Now, I'm not including hybrid here, but I'm just going to use this to show you how much incrementally selling product can help you out and why it was such a big part of our businesses when I had my gyms, because I knew that I was literally cash flowing my entire campaign, just from product sales, let alone all of the services we'd be selling on top of it. All right. And so if you're making $315 ahead, now, we also know that they're going, we're going to get more from that from lifetime value because most people are going to take subscription on these products because they're going to be being, they're going to be with you for longer than four weeks, no matter what. Right. So you're going to get multiple turns on these subscriptions. Okay. Again, 42% margin, which is three times what most gyms are running, which means that you can spend an additional $120 per acquisition, not including the three and a half turns. I just say that just to point it out, but just in the immediate cash value of the customer, you can spend extra $120 per acquisition, which breaks down to $24 per lead for immediate cash value. Okay. And that does not include the front end sale. If you do include the front end service sale, then you'd be able to make it $44 per lead. So simply by adding this one thing, you more than double how much you can spend per customer. Now, when you compare that to how much hybrid ads, it seems like it's inconsequential, but one lesson that I've learned from a mentor was never put the word just in front of money. All right. Never put the word just in front of money. These are, this is extra revenue that you can bring in to decrease risk and to pay your people better and to service your clients better and to ultimately provide more value to them than you would otherwise. And if you are not doing it, you are, you are being an inferior business owner. That's all I'm going to say. Like it's being, you're being an inferior business owner. And so this is like, if you're paying us to help you win, let us help you win. You want to implement this little strategy of the no sale sale for people who say no in the beginning. So you can bring them back and still monetize the person, every person who walks to the door. And then also for the people who do say yes to buying service from you, you can even more maximize that relationship by selling them something else. And I want to break this belief for you again, them spending more money on product in no way to track from how much the money they will spend on service. Okay, it is a different wallet is a different buying decision altogether. VIMS buying stuff for me from the gym does not impact how much they spend on groceries. I'm telling you, it's a different budget. It's a different buying decision. And so the more of those buying decisions we can ultimately capture, the more we can monetize, the more ways we can serve customers. Okay, so I just like to point this one out by not even including how much you're making from hybrid, just to show you how much more you can make just by adding that upsell in it. And so I need you to be very mindful of what an optimized selling process looks like because those are the things that are going to help you outcompete and outlast any competitor in your space and be able to outspend them in the acquisition so that you can use more platforms and ultimately reach and help more people.
